1910s.title: BLS CPI-U series begins
1910s.body: The United States data starts in 1913. World War I and postwar adjustment pushed prices higher, so a dollar from this period lost purchasing power quickly.

1930s.title: U.S. depression deflation
1930s.body: U.S. prices fell during the early Great Depression, raising the measured purchasing power of dollars while unemployment and business failures made conditions severe.

1970s.title: U.S. inflation accelerates
1970s.body: Oil shocks, policy strain, and the end of dollar-gold convertibility contributed to a steep erosion of U.S. purchasing power through the 1970s.

2020s.title: U.S. prices reprice after COVID
2020s.body: The early 2020s brought the fastest U.S. price increases in decades, driven by supply disruption, reopening demand, energy costs, and policy support.
